Name: Blaine's Moltres
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, shuffle Blaine's Moltres and all cards attached to it into your deck (after doing damage).

Name: Brock's Rhydon
Ability-Effect: As long as Brock's Rhydon is Benched, whenever 1 of your Benched Pokémon is damaged, you may do 10 of that damage to Brock's Rhydon instead. (If more than 1 of your Benched Pokémon is damaged at the same time, you may use this power once for each of them. )
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Erika's Dragonair
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )
Shuffle Erika's Dragonair and all cards attached to it into your deck. Then, your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

Name: Erika's Clefable
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, you may return any number of your Pokémon in play and all cards attached to them to your hand.

Name: Erika's Vileplume
Ability-Effect: If an attack does damage to Erika's Vileplume while it's your Active Pokémon (even if it's Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. This power works even while Erika's Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: If Erika's Vileplume does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove a number of damage counters from Erika's Vileplume equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Erika's Vileplume has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

Name: Lt. Surge's Electabuzz
Effects: Take up to 2 [L] Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz.

Discard all [L] Energy cards attached to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz in order to use this attack. Flip a number of coins equal to the number of [L] Energy cards you discarded. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Lt. Surge's Fearow
Effects: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Lt. Surge's Magneton
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), if Lt. Surge's Magneton is your Active Pokémon, you may take 1 [L] Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to Lt. Surge's Magneton. This power can't be used if Lt. Surge's Magneton is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, Lt. Surge's Magneton does 20 damage to itself.

Name: Misty's Seadra
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 60 more damage. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does 30 damage.

Name: Misty's Tentacruel
Ability-Effect: If an attack does damage to Misty's Tentacruel while it's your Active Pokémon, you may switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, which prevents all other effects of that attack on Misty's Tentacruel. This power can't be used if Misty's Tentacruel is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Rocket's Hitmonchan
Effects: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Hitmonchan is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, Rocket's Hitmonchan attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for double that amount of damage. (If Rocket's Hitmonchan takes 20 damage, it does 40 damage to that Pokémon. )

Name: Rocket's Moltres
Ability-Effect: When Rocket's Moltres is Knocked Out, you may return it to your hand after discarding it. This power can't be used if Rocket's Moltres is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when it is Knocked Out.
Effects: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Moltres during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Moltres is Knocked Out), Rocket's Moltres attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for 10 damage. (Apply Weakness and Resistance. )

Name: Rocket's Scyther
Effects: Whenever Rocket's Scyther is attacked, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does no damage to Rocket's Scyther. (Any other effects of the attack still happen. ) This effect lasts until Rocket's Scyther takes damage (or is Benched or is evolved).

Name: Sabrina's Gengar
Effects: Put a damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon has already has any damage counters on it.
Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

Name: Brock
Description: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

Name: Erika
Description: Each player may draw up to 3 cards. You draw first.

Name: Lt. Surge
Description: You can play this card only if you have at least 1 Basic Pokémon card in your hand. Put a Basic Pokémon card from your hand into play as your Active Pokémon. Put your old Active Pokémon onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full. )

Name: Misty
Description: Discard 2 of the other cards in your hand in order to play this card. If this turn's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and if the attacking Pokémon has Misty in its name, the attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Brock's Golem
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose up to 3 of them. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: The Rocket's Trap
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, choose up to 3 cards at random from your opponent's hand (don't look at them). Your opponent shuffles those cards into his or her deck.

Name: Brock's Rhyhorn
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. )
Brock's Rhyhorn does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Brock's Onix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose up to 2 of them. This attack does 20 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. ) Brock's Onix can't attack during your next turn.

Name: Brock's Zubat
Effects: Draw a card. Then, switch Brock's Zubat with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. You can't use this attack if your Bench is empty.

Name: Brock's Sandslash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

Name: Erika's Clefairy
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Erika's Victreebel
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Erika's Victreebel is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

Name: Lt. Surge's Electabuzz
Effects: Take up to 2 [L] Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz.
Take 1 [L] Energy card attached to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you have no Benched Pokémon, discard that Energy card.

Name: Lt. Surge's Raichu
Effects: Discard all Energy cards attached to Lt. Surge's Raichu in order to use this attack.

Name: Misty's Cloyster
Ability-Effect: You may reduce all damage done by attacks to Misty's Cloyster by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Any other effects of attacks still happen). This power can't be used if Misty's Cloyster is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Misty's Goldeen
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Misty's Poliwrath
Effects: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon that isn't [W] on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Misty's Tentacool
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Search your deck for any number of Pokémon named Tentacool, Tentacruel, Misty's Tentacool, and/or Misty's Tentacruel. Show those cards to your opponent, then put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Rocket's Snorlax
Ability-Effect: If your opponent's attack does damage to Rocket's Snorlax and Rocket's Snorlax is already Asleep (even if it's Knocked Out), this power does 20 damage to the attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Rocket's Snorlax is now Asleep (after doing damage).

Name: Sabrina's Venomoth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. If a Pokémon has fewer damage counters than the number of heads, remove all of them.
Flip 2 coins. If 1 or both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Blaine's Growlithe
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. )
Blaine's Growlithe does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Blaine's Kangaskhan
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

Name: Blaine's Magmar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. (If there are fewer than 5 cards in your deck, discard all of them. ) This attack does 20 damage for each [R] Energy card you discarded in this way.

Name: Brock's Geodude
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. ) This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

Name: Brock's Graveler
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Brock's Golbat
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

Name: Erika's Dratini
Ability-Effect: Whenever an attack by a Basic Pokémon (including your own) does 20 or more damage to Erika's Dratini (after applying Weakness and Resistance), reduce that damage to 10. (Any other effects of attacks still happen. ) This power stops working while Erika's Dratini is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

Name: Brock's Lickitung
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Erika's Exeggcute
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, whenever Erika's Exeggcute takes damage, divide that damage in half (rounded down to the nearest 10). (Any other effects still happen. )
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing to the Defending Pokémon and Erika's Exeggcute does 20 damage to itself.

Name: Erika's Exeggutor
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 5 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

Name: Erika's Gloom
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 4 damage counters from Erika's Gloom. If Erika's Gloom has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned (your choice).

Name: Erika's Gloom
Effects: Both the Defending Pokémon and Erika's Gloom are now Asleep (after doing damage).
Both the Defending Pokémon and Erika's Gloom are now Confused (after doing damage).

Name: Erika's Oddish
Ability-Effect: All Energy cards attached to Erika's Oddish provide [G] Energy instead of their usual type. This power works even while Erika's Oddish is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Erika's Weepinbell
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

Name: Erika's Weepinbell
Effects: Before doing damage, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

Name: Lt. Surge's Raticate
Effects: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

Name: Lt. Surge's Magnemite
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Misty's Psyduck
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon with Misty in its name and put it onto your Bench. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full. ) Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Misty's Seaking
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Misty's Starmie
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Misty's Starmie but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Ex-Rule: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Misty's Poliwhirl
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.
Flip a number of coins equal to the number of [W] Energy attached to Misty's Poliwhirl. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 damage for each heads.

Name: Sabrina's Haunter
Effects: Flip a number of coins equal to the total number of Sabrina's Gastlys, Sabrina's Haunters, and Sabrina's Gengars you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Misty's Tentacool
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach Energy cards to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

Name: Sabrina's Jynx
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If the Defending Pokémon was Asleep, it is no longer Asleep.

Name: Sabrina's Slowbro
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 3 damage counters from Sabrina's Slowbro and Sabrina's Slowbro is now Asleep. If Sabrina's Slowbro has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.
You can't use this attack during your next turn.

Name: Blaine's Growlithe
Effects: Does 10 damage to each [G] Pokémon on your opponent's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Blaine's Charmander
Effects: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Blaine's Charmander in order to use this attack. If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

Name: Blaine's Tauros
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads to the Defending Pokémon and 20 damage times the number of tails to Blaine's Tauros.

Name: Blaine's Ponyta
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Blaine's Ponyta.

Name: Blaine's Vulpix
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from Blaine's Vulpix. This power can't be used if Blaine's Vulpix is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Brock's Geodude
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card with Brock in its name and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full. )
Don't apply Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Resistance still happen. )

Name: Brock's Mankey
Effects: Shuffle your deck.
Does 40 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Brock's Mankey.

Name: Brock's Mankey
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Brock's Onix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Brock's Rhyhorn
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Brock's Sandshrew
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Brock's Sandshrew
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Brock's Sandshrew during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen. )

Name: Brock's Zubat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Brock's Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

Name: Erika's Bellsprout
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Erika's Bellsprout
Effects: Erika's Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Erika's Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Erika's Exeggcute. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Erika's Oddish
Effects: If there are any damage counters on Erika's Oddish, remove 1 of them.
If Erika's Oddish has any damage counters on it, flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 of those damage counters.

Name: Erika's Tangela
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Lt. Surge's Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Lt. Surge's Pikachu
Effects: Take 1 [L] Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to Lt. Surge's Pikachu.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Lt. Surge's Rattata
Effects: During your next turn, Lt. Surge's Rattata's Gnaw attack's base damage is doubled.

Name: Lt. Surge's Spearow
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. )
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Lt. Surge's Voltorb
Effects: Flip 1 coin. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Misty's Goldeen
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Misty's Horsea
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

Name: Misty's Poliwag
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Misty's Shellder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

Name: Misty's Staryu
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Sabrina's Abra
Effects: Return a [P] Energy card attached to Sabrina's Abra to your hand in order to use this attack.

Name: Sabrina's Drowzee
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Sabrina's Gastly
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Sabrina's Slowpoke
Effects: Sabrina's Slowpoke is now Asleep (after doing damage).

Name: Sabrina's Venonat
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.

Name: Blaine's Quiz #1
Description: Put a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent its name. Your opponent guesses the length of that Pokémon. Flip the card over. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 2 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 2 cards. Either way, return the card to your hand.

Name: Sabrina's Mr. Mime
Effects: Choose up to 3 cards from your hand and put them on top of your deck. Then search your deck for that many basic Energy cards. Show those cards to your opponent, then put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Brock
Description: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

Name: Charity
Description: Attach Charity to your Active Pokémon. Unless that Pokémon gets Knocked Out, return Charity to your hand at the end of your turn. If that Pokémon attacks and does damage to the Defending Pokémon, you may reduce that damage by any amount (rounded to the nearest 10).

Name: Erika
Description: Each player may draw up to 3 cards. You draw first.

Name: Lt. Surge
Description: You can play this card only if you have at least 1 Basic Pokémon card in your hand. Put a Basic Pokémon card from your hand into play as your Active Pokémon. Put your old Active Pokémon onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full. )

Name: No Removal Gym
Description: A player must discard 2 cards from his or her hand in order to play an Energy Removal or Super Energy Removal card.

Name: Misty
Description: Discard 2 of the other cards in your hand in order to play this card. If this turn's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and if the attacking Pokémon has Misty in its name, the attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: The Rocket's Training Gym
Description: Each player pays [C] more to retreat his or her Active Pokémon.

Name: Blaine's Last Resort
Description: You can't play this card if you have any cards in your hand other than Blaine's Last Resort. Show your hand to your opponent, then draw 5 cards.

Name: Brock's Training Method
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card with Brock in its name. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Celadon City Gym
Description: During each player's turn, that player may choose to discard an Energy card attached to 1 of his or her Pokémon with Erika in its name. If that player does so, that Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

Name: Cerulean City Gym
Description: Each player pays [C] less to retreat his or her Pokémon if it has Misty in its name.

Name: Erika's Maids
Description: Trade 2 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards with Erika in their names from your deck. Show those cards to your opponent, then put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Good Manners
Description: In order to play this card, you can't have any Basic Pokémon cards in your hand. Show your hand to your opponent, then search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Erika's Perfume
Description: Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Basic Pokémon cards there, you may put any number of them onto your opponent's Bench (as long as there's room).

Name: Lt. Surge's Treaty
Description: Your opponent chooses 1 of the following: everyone chooses 1 of his or her own Prizes and put it into his or her hand, or you draw a card.

Name: Minion of Team Rocket
Description: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and return it and all cards attached to it to his or her hand. If 1 or both of them are tails, your turn ends immediately (you can't attack this turn).

Name: Misty's Wrath
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose 2 of those cards and put them into your hand. Discard the rest.

Name: Pewter City Gym
Description: Don't apply Resistance to any attacks made by Pokémon with Brock in their names.

Name: Recall
Description: For your attack this turn, your Active Pokémon can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon card or any Evolution card attached to it. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost. )

Name: Sabrina's ESP
Description: Attach Sabrina's ESP to 1 of your Pokémon with Sabrina in its name. At the end of your turn, discard Sabrina's ESP. If that Pokémon uses and attack that involves flipping coins, Sabrina's ESP lets you re-flip those coins once. If you do, re-flip all the coins.

Name: Secret Mission
Description: Look at your opponent's hand. Then, you may discard as many other cards as you want from your hand and draw that many cards.

Name: Tickling Machine
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent sets aside all the cards in his or her hand face down. Nobody may look at those cards. At the end of your opponent's next turn, your opponent puts those cards back into his or her hand. If tails, your turn ends immediately (you can't attack this turn).

Name: Vermilion City Gym
Description: Whenever a player attacks with a Pokémon with Lt. Surge in its name, he or she may flip a coin. If heads, and if that Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, the attacking Pokémon does 10 damage to itself in addition to whatever its attack usually does.

Name: Blaine's Gamble
Description: Discard any number of other cards from your hand, then flip a coin. If heads, draw twice that many cards.

Name: Energy Flow
Description: For each of your Pokémon, you may return any number of Energy cards attached to it to your hand.

Name: Misty's Duel
Description: You and your opponent play a game of Rock-Paper-Scissors. The winner shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws a new hand of 5 cards. (If you don't know how to play Rock-Paper-Scissors, flip a coin to decide who's the winner. )

Name: Narrow Gym
Description: No player may have more than 4 Pokémon on his or her Bench. When this card is played, if a player has 5 Pokémon on his or her Bench, that player chooses 1 of them and returns it and all cards attached to it to his or her hand. (If both players have to return a Pokémon, your opponent returns a Pokémon first. )

Name: Sabrina's Gaze
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws a new hand of the same number of cards.

Name: Trash Exchange
Description: Count the number of cards in your discard pile and shuffle them into your deck. Then discard that many cards from the top of your deck.
